DownTown Angels
Sponsored
Why does a loving God allow pain and suffering in the world?

By Neil McBride

This is one of the most profound and persistent questions spiritual seekers ask today. Many wrestles with a difficult paradox in a world marked by pain, injustice, and uncertainty: If God is truly all-powerful and all-loving, why does suffering exist? In today’s blog, we’ll explore this timeless dilemma, not by offering simplistic answers, but by thoughtfully examining the nature of divine power, love, and the deeper purpose that might lie behind human suffering. Is it possible that God is omnipotent and compassionate, yet still allows pain? Or is there something more we’re meant to understand? Let’s delve into it.

Suffering as a Consequence of a Broken World

The Bible begins with a picture of peace and perfection—creation declared “very good” by God in Genesis. But with the gift of human freedom came the potential for disobedience. When Adam and Eve sinned (Genesis 3), the harmony of creation was shattered. Sin brought suffering, death, and separation from God. Suffering was never part of God's original intent but entered as a result of humanity’s fall. As Romans 8:22 (KJV) says, “For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.”

God’s Power and Presence Amid Suffering

God does not remain distant in the face of human pain. In the Old Testament, He declares in Exodus 3:7 (KJV), “I have surely seen the affliction of my people… and have heard their cry.” In the New Testament, God enters human history through Jesus Christ, “a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief” (Isaiah 53:3, KJV). At the cross, we witness divine love and power united. Christ did not remain removed from suffering—He bore it. The crucifixion is not a workaround; it is God's personal response to evil. He suffers with us and for us.

Suffering with a Purpose

While Scripture does not provide simple explanations for suffering, it reveals that God can work through it. Romans 5:3–5 (KJV) teaches that “tribulation worketh patience; And patience, experience; and experience, hope.” James 1:2–4 (KJV) urges believers to “count it all joy… when ye fall into divers temptations; Knowing this, that the trying of your faith worketh patience.” Though suffering is painful, God uses it to shape character and deepen faith. As Joseph said in Genesis 50:20 (KJV), “Ye thought evil against me; but God meant it unto good.”

The Hope of Restoration

The Bible’s story ends not in despair, but in renewal. Revelation 21:4 (KJV) offers this glorious promise: “And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain.” This is not fantasy or escape, but the Christian hope—the restoration of all things. In the end, God will make everything right, and His people will dwell in a new creation where justice and peace reign forever.

God's Power Knows No Bounds

The Bible repeatedly emphasises this truth; God is called “Almighty” fifty-eight times. Psalm 147:5 (KJV) declares, “Great is our Lord, and of great power: his understanding is infinite.” Jeremiah 32:17 (KJV) echoes this, saying, “Ah Lord GOD! behold, thou hast made the heaven and the earth by thy great power and stretched out arm, and there is nothing too hard for thee.”

From the creation of the heavens to sustaining all things, God’s power knows no bounds. Nothing is too great for Him or beyond His reach. As 1 Chronicles 29:11, Jeremiah 10:12, and Colossians 1:16 (KJV) also confirm, His power is unmatched and the foundation upon which all things exist.

God’s Infinite Strength

The Bible leaves no doubt that God is all-powerful. He is called “Almighty” fifty-eight times throughout Scripture. Psalm 147:5 (KJV) declares, “Great is our Lord, and of great power: his understanding is infinite.” Jeremiah 32:17 (KJV) echoes this truth: “Ah Lord GOD! behold, thou hast made the heaven and the earth by thy great power and stretched out arm, and there is nothing too hard for thee.”

From the vastness of the universe to the smallest detail of life, all creation exists and is sustained by His power. As 1 Chronicles 29:11, Jeremiah 10:12, and Colossians 1:16 (KJV) confirm, God’s strength upholds everything. There is no limit to His might, and nothing is beyond His ability.

God's Everlasting Goodness

Just as God is powerful, the Bible also clarifies that He is perfectly good. He is loving, merciful, and full of compassion. 1 Chronicles 16:34 (KJV) proclaims, “O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever.” Psalm 86:5 (KJV) says, “For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.” And Psalm 145:9 (KJV) affirms, “The LORD is good to all: and his tender mercies are over all his works.”

God’s goodness is not limited to a few; it extends to all He has made. He is not only able to help us with His power, but also willing because He is good. His mercy is everlasting, and His love reaches everyone who calls upon Him.

God is all good and all loving, and His mercies endure forever

If the Bible clearly teaches that God is good and He is powerful, then how do we explain evil in the world? The Apostle Paul wrote in 1 Corinthians 13:12 (KJV), “For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.” What we see and understand on this side of heaven is like looking through a fog. We can make out the edges, but we do not see things clearly. We do not have all the details, but one day, we will.

So, where do pain and suffering come from? When you get an “F” on your report card, that’s not a good thing. Three “F’s” would be even worse. The Bible tells us that our world has three “F’s” that cause us to fail, and these are the sources of suffering around us:
Free Choice: In Genesis 1-2, God created the world and placed man in it. Man was given the freedom to choose to obey God and enjoy His blessing, or to disobey God and suffer the consequences. Unfortunately, man freely chose to disobey God, and in that moment, sin entered into the world, and everything changed. The freedom to choose brings with it the freedom to choose both rightly and wrongly. You might ask, “Why didn’t God create a world where we would choose what’s right every time?” He certainly could have, but then there would be no true love or fellowship. Love is a choice. Without the freedom to choose to obey and to love, this world would be robotic and loveless.

Fallen World: God created the world good, but then sin permeated it, and we now live in a world that is much different than the world God created. Romans 3:23 (KJV) says, “For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.” We live in a fallen world, and this “fallenness” affects everything — our thinking, our culture, our behavior, and our sense of right and wrong. This is why we see abuse, addiction, perversion, the killing of the innocent, rape, incest, and all manner of evil. This “fallenness” also affects even the natural world around us. Diseases like cancer, heart disease, Alzheimer’s, and mental illness are all the effects of sin in the world. Natural disasters like tornadoes, tsunamis, hurricanes, and earthquakes are the reverberations of sin in our natural world. Romans 8 tells us that our world is corrupted by sin and is groaning, weakening, and struggling under the weight of it.

Forces of Evil: There is a real, unseen spiritual world of evil that empowers and emboldens evil in our world. Ephesians 6:12 (KJV) says, “For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places.” Mass shootings, terrorist killings, and preoccupations with the occult are all expressions of evil motivated by the unseen forces of evil around us.

God’s Infinite Wisdom

While the Bible asserts that God is all-powerful and all-loving, it also declares that God is all-wise. Psalm 147:5 (KJV) says, “Great is our Lord, and of great power: his understanding is infinite.” Romans 16:27 (KJV) declares Him “the only wise God.” Even Job, who suffered more than anyone, confessed, “With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.” (Job 12:13 KJV).

Isaiah 55:8-9 (KJV) reminds us, “For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, saith the Lord. As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts more than your thoughts.” And Romans 8:28 (KJV) assures us, “And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are called according to his purpose.”

When Satan sought to destroy Jesus and make Him suffer on the cross, God worked even that for our good and His glory. Though we all endure suffering, we can find comfort in God’s presence. The Scripture promises that God is moved when we suffer. Psalm 34:18 (KJV) says, “The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.” Jesus Himself intercedes for us. Romans 8:34 (KJV) declares, “It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.”

Ultimately, God will put an end to suffering once and for all. Romans 8:18 (KJV) gives us this hope: “For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.” One day, Jesus is coming again, and Revelation 21:4 (KJV) promises, “And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain.”

Until then, we look to Jesus, trusting Him to give us the strength to overcome. John 16:33 (KJV) assures us, “These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.”

God, the Source of All Wisdom

In addition to being powerful and loving, God is perfectly wise. Psalm 147:5 (KJV) says, “His understanding is infinite.” And Romans 16:27 (KJV) calls Him “To God only wise, be glory through Jesus Christ for ever.”

Even Job, who endured great suffering, confessed in Job 12:13 (KJV):
“With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.”

We may not understand why suffering is allowed, but God does. As Isaiah 55:8-9 (KJV) says:
“For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, saith the LORD.”
Yet, in His wisdom, God is working all things, even pain, for good to those who trust Him.

Romans 8:28 (KJV) says:
“And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God and are called according to his purpose.”
Even the cross, the greatest act of injustice in history, became the very means of our salvation. What Satan meant for evil, God used for good and glory.

God’s Comfort in Our Suffering

God does not ignore our pain. Psalm 34:18 (KJV) tells us:
“The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.”

Jesus Himself is our intercessor. Romans 8:34 (KJV) says:
“It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.”

Our suffering is real, but it is not the end of the story. Romans 8:18 (KJV) gives us this hope:
“For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory which shall be revealed in us.”

And one day, God will bring a full and final end to all pain. Revelation 21:4 (KJV) promises:

And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain.”

Until that day, we hold on to the words of Jesus in John 16:33 (KJV):
“In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.”

Conclusion

God allows suffering not because He is weak or uncaring, but because He is powerful, loving, and wise. He gave us freedom, and with it, the possibility of wrong choices. But he also gives us grace every time we fall.

Yes, He could stop all pain, but to do so would mean removing our ability to choose. Instead, He walks with us through our pain, shaping us through it and promising to bring something beautiful from our brokenness. Ultimately, every tear will be wiped away, and every wrong will be made right.

DTA – Neil McBride
(CEO and founder of Downtown Angels)
@top fans
Kingdom Fellowship
Church, Organization

ReelShort
Sponsored
리암과 애니는 누구보다 사랑했던 연인이었다. 그러나 애니를 지키려다 리암이 감옥에 가면서 두 사람의 인생은 갈라지고 만다. 그 순간, 부유한 홀랜드 가문의 수장은 리암이 상속자임을 밝히며, 그의 자유를 대가로 애니에게 떠날 것을 강요한다. 애니는 사랑하는 이를 위해 모든 진실을 숨기고 떠나며, 자신의 임신 사실까지 감춘다. 6년이 흘러, 애니는 아들 헨리와 함께 조용히 살아가지만, 과거의 상처는 그녀를 놓아주지 않는다. 한편, 홀랜드 가문의 상속자가 된 리암은 애니를 향한 배신감과 여전한 사랑으로 괴로워한다. 운명은 두 사람을 다시 만나게 하고, 리암은 애니를 붙잡으려 하지만, 그녀는 헨리를 지키기 위해 그를 밀어낸다.리암은 애니와 헨리를 되찾기 위해 모든 걸 걸기로 결심한다. 과연 그는 과거의 벽을 허물고, 잃어버린 사랑과 가족을 되찾을 수 있을까?

Stephanie Robertson
Sponsored
I didn't want to share this because it's pretty personal – but if it helps even one person my dad's age finally breathe through the night again, it's worth it.

My dad has suffered from severe sleep apnea for 8 years. It started when he turned 60 – the snoring got so bad that Mom moved to the guest room.

Then came the breathing stops.

The gasping.

Those terrifying moments when he completely stopped breathing – like watching someone drowning in their own bed.

He tried everything.

CPAP machine... (he couldn't stand it – ripped it off every night)

Dental appliances...

Lost a good amount of weight...

But nothing really worked.

He was getting by on 3-4 hours of broken sleep, waking up gasping over 20 times per night.

But last month, everything changed.

I was visiting my parents when I heard a crash at 3 AM.

I found Dad leaning against the hallway wall – he'd gotten up dizzy and disoriented from oxygen deprivation and lost his balance.

But stubborn as he is, he insisted everything was fine and said, "I just stood up too fast."

Mom and I dragged him to the ER anyway. The doctor didn't sugarcoat it:

"Your father's heart is working overtime from the constant oxygen drops. Untreated sleep apnea at this stage can lead to stroke or heart attack."

Mom burst into tears. Dad just looked... defeated. Like someone had pulled the plug on his last bit of hope.

Two weeks later, we took a family trip to Miami Beach. Mom thought a change of scenery would do Dad good. We splurged on a nice hotel in South Beach.

First night at the hotel – something strange happened.

Dad lay down around 7 PM to "rest before dinner."

He didn't wake up until 7 AM the next morning.

Mom actually shook him awake because she panicked – he was breathing so calm and steady.

No snoring. No gasping. No breathing stops.

When he finally woke up, he sat up confused.

"I... I can breathe through my nose," he said, taking a deep breath. "I didn't wake up once."

The rest of the trip, he slept like he was 20 again.

By day three, his face had color again.

He wasn't nodding off at lunch anymore.

He could keep up with my kids on the beach.

Before we left, I begged the front desk clerk to tell me about their pillows. She tried to brush me off until I explained Dad's situation.

"It's a Neck Therapy Pillow," she finally admitted. "But it's a specialized medical pillow – you can't find it in regular stores."

Wrong. I found that pillow online that same night.

The product photos showed an unusual butterfly shape – a deep contour in the middle, raised areas on the sides. Nothing like Dad's "orthopedic" pillow from the medical supply store.

The description talked about a "3-Zone Support System for optimal cervical spine positioning."

Turns out, this pillow was specifically engineered to address the true cause of snoring and sleep apnea – the position of your neck, head, and chin during sleep.

Here's the thing: when your neck isn't properly supported during sleep, your airway collapses like a garden hose with a kink in it – this blocks airflow, causes snoring, and triggers breathing stops. Your tongue falls back, your soft palate drops, and suddenly you're suffocating in your sleep.

The ergonomic shape of this pillow keeps your neck, head, and chin in optimal position all night, so your airway stays wide open. Think of it like opening a valve that's been partially closed for years – suddenly air can flow freely again.

I ordered two pillows.

When they arrived, Dad was skeptical. "A pillow can't fix eight years of sleep apnea."

But the first morning after using it, he came into the kitchen making pancakes.

Awake.

Energized.

Like the old Dad I remembered.

Mom started crying. But this time they were good tears.

"You didn't snore once," she whispered. "Not a single time."

It's been two months now.

Dad sleeps 7-8 hours straight every night.

His sleep tracker showed his apnea episodes dropped from 24 per hour to 6 – mild range, no longer dangerous.

The snoring? Gone.

The gasping? Vanished.

The terrifying breathing stops? Don't happen anymore.

It was like someone had cleared the blockage from a clogged pipe – suddenly everything just flowed the way it was supposed to.

His cardiologist was so shocked she asked what had changed.

"My daughter got me a new pillow," he said.

She laughed... until she saw his latest heart readings. Then she asked for the website.

I'm sharing this story because I know someone reading this has a dad suffering exactly like mine did.

Watching your dad – the strongest person you know – literally suffocate in his sleep every night is heartbreaking. It's like watching a car slowly run out of gas with no gas station in sight.

The Neck Therapy Pillow works because it's not just a pillow – it's engineered to maintain proper neck, head, and chin positioning.

Think about first aid – the first thing you do is tilt the head back to open the airways. This pillow does that automatically, all night long.

The Neck Therapy Pillow isn't cheap (although they're running a 40% off sale right now).

But compared to the $1,500 CPAP machine collecting dust?

Or another ER visit?

Or finding him blue-faced one morning?

It's worth every penny.

They even have a 30-night trial period. So just order one.

If your dad (or you) doesn't breathe better, send it back.

But I bet you'll order more, like I did. I got one for myself and my husband who "barely snores" – except now he doesn't snore at all, and I'm back in our bedroom after 2 years of separate sleeping.

My advice: Don't wait for a nighttime emergency. And don't wait until oxygen deprivation causes permanent damage.

I've included the link below.

https://try.pillowdaddy-us.com/advert-1-neck-therapy-pillow-snoring

P.S. - Quick warning: There are knockoffs all over Amazon that look similar but don't have the actual neck-positioning design. They're just memory foam pillows with weird shapes.

So get the real Neck Therapy Pillow from the official site – I've included the link below. My friend learned this the hard way when she tried to save $20. That cheap, fake pillow made her dad's snoring even worse.

Here's the discount link: https://try.pillowdaddy-us.com/advert-1-neck-therapy-pillow-snoring
TRY.PILLOWDADDY-US.COM

Klaserie Drift Safari Camps
Sponsored
Planning your South African safari but want something beyond the typical brochure experience?

Most safari marketing looks identical: the same Big Five promises, the same sunset photos, the same carefully scripted experiences.

At Klaserie Drift Safari, we focus on what brochures can't capture: the unscripted moments when a leopard decides to rest in shade near your suite, or when your ranger stops mid-drive because fresh elephant tracks tell a story worth following. 🐘

Picture mornings where your itinerary follows wildlife rhythms, not predetermined schedules.

Your ranger shares knowledge passed down through generations of working in this specific piece of Greater Kruger.

When elephants linger at the waterhole below your deck, you linger too.

No rushing to the next "must-see" attraction because this moment belongs to you and the African bush.

Ready for your authentic African summer?

🌿 Real wilderness moments can't be manufactured or guaranteed, only experienced.
KLASERIEDRIFT.CO.ZA
Beyond The Brochure Safari
Step into the Greater Kruger, where authentic wilderness still thrives. Far from the crowds, your safari feels deeply personal - and your stay directly supports the protection of this extraordinary ecosystem.
